title: 11 Best Herbal Teas For High Cholesterol
intro: Herbal teas for high cholesterol are a natural and holistic approach to reducing cholesterol levels in the blood. They are a type of tea made from herbs, plants, and flowers that have been traditionally used to lower cholesterol levels and improve cardiovascular health. Some examples of herbal teas that can help treat high cholesterol include ginseng tea, which helps to improve circulation and lower triglycerides, turmeric tea, which reduces inflammation and improves lipid profiles, and ginger tea, which helps to reduce blood pressure and improve blood flow. Other herbal teas such as dandelion tea, peppermint tea, and green tea have also been shown to help lower cholesterol levels, improve cardiovascular health, and reduce the risk of heart disease.

preparations.herb_desc: Ginkgo biloba teas have been traditionally used to help manage high cholesterol levels due to its potential ability to lower LDL cholesterol and increase HDL cholesterol. This herbal preparation contains bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, terpenoids, and bilobalide, which are believed to contribute to its cholesterol-lowering effects. Ginkgo biloba teas may help to treat high cholesterol by improving blood lipid profiles, reducing inflammation, and enhancing the body's antioxidant defenses, ultimately leading to a decrease in the risk of cardiovascular disease. Regular consumption of Ginkgo biloba teas may provide benefits in reducing total cholesterol levels, improving blood circulation, and enhancing overall cardiovascular health.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 teaspoon of dried Ginkgo biloba leaves, 1 cup of boiling water, and a heat-resistant cup.
Place the Ginkgo biloba leaves in the heat-resistant cup.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the Ginkgo biloba leaves.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the liquid into another cup.
Drink the Ginkgo biloba tea 2-3 times a day, as part of a healthy diet and lifestyle, to help manage high cholesterol.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Ginkgo biloba teas can be used to treat high cholesterol, but it may cause side effects such as stomach upset, nausea, and diarrhea in some individuals. When using Ginkgo biloba teas to treat high cholesterol, it is essential to take precautions to avoid interactions with blood thinners, such as aspirin or warfarin, and to be aware of potential allergic reactions, as well as to start with a low dose and gradually increase it to minimize side effects.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Ginkgo biloba tea daily to support cholesterol health.
Take 250-500 mg of Ginkgo biloba extract 2-3 times a day.
Monitor your cholesterol levels and adjust Ginkgo biloba dosage as needed.
Combine Ginkgo biloba tea with a balanced diet and regular exercise for best results.
Consult a healthcare professional before making Ginkgo biloba tea a regular part of your routine.

preparations.herb_desc: Silybum marianum teas, also known as milk thistle tea, have been traditionally used to treat high cholesterol levels due to their properties that help lower lipid profiles and reduce inflammation in the body. The bioactive constituents of silymarin, a flavonoid complex found in milk thistle tea, have been shown to inhibit the absorption of dietary fats and cholesterol in the gut, thereby reducing the risk of atherosclerosis and cardiovascular diseases. By inhibiting the activity of HMG-CoA reductase, an enzyme involved in cholesterol synthesis, silymarin helps to decrease cholesterol production in the liver, resulting in improved lipid profiles and reduced cardiovascular risk. The benefits of using milk thistle tea to treat high cholesterol include reduced triglycerides, improved HDL cholesterol levels, and a lower risk of cardiovascular complications, making it a potential natural remedy for this common health issue.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 cups of fresh Silybum marianum flowers, also known as Milk Thistle.
Dry the flowers in a low-temperature oven (150°F - 200°F) for 1-2 hours, or air-dry them for 1 week.
Measure 1 tablespoon of dried Silybum marianum flowers and place them in a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the herbs and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and drink it warm, 2-3 times a day, as needed to help manage high cholesterol.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Silybum marianum teas can be used to treat high cholesterol, but its use may be associated with side effects such as nausea, diarrhea, and stomach upset, as well as interactions with certain medications that may affect blood sugar levels or blood thinning. When using Silybum marianum teas to treat high cholesterol, it's essential to take precautions, such as starting with a low dose and gradually increasing it to minimize stomach upset and potential allergic reactions, and to avoid using it simultaneously with medications like statins or blood thinners.
preparation_usage: Consult doctor before using Silybum marianum tea for high cholesterol.
Drink 2-3 cups of Silybum marianum tea daily for 2-3 months.
Start with 1 cup per day and increase gradually to 3 cups.
Take Silybum marianum tea 2-3 times a day after meals.
Monitor cholesterol levels and adjust tea consumption as needed.

preparations.herb_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis teas, derived from the leaves of the rosemary plant, have been traditionally used to treat high cholesterol levels due to their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. These herbal teas help to treat high cholesterol by promoting cardiovascular health, improving lipid profiles, and reducing inflammation in the body. The bioactive constituents of Rosmarinus officinalis, including carnosic acid, ursolic acid, and rosmarinic acid, are responsible for its therapeutic effects, which include inhibiting the production of cholesterol in the liver and improving blood lipid profiles. Regular consumption of Rosmarinus officinalis teas has been shown to offer benefits in reducing total cholesterol and LDL (bad) cholesterol levels, thereby reducing the risk of heart disease and stroke.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Rosmarinus officinalis leaves or 2 teaspoons of dried leaves.
Heat 1 cup of water in a pot until it boils.
Add the Rosmarinus officinalis leaves to the boiling water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a piece of cheesecloth or a tea strainer.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help lower high cholesterol levels.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Rosmarinus officinalis teas can be an effective herbal remedy for high cholesterol, but it may cause side effects such as allergic reactions, digestive issues, and dizziness in some individuals. To use Rosmarinus officinalis teas safely, it is recommended to start with a small dose, avoid consuming it before bedtime, and monitor your blood pressure and heart rate closely, especially if you have a history of cardiovascular conditions.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Rosmarinus officinalis tea 2 times a day.
Take 1 tablespoon of dried leaves in a cup of boiling water.
Steep for 5-10 minutes and let the mixture cool.
Consume within 2-3 weeks, then take a 1-week break.
Repeat the cycle for 3-6 months for optimal results.

preparations.herb_desc: Zingiber officinale teas, derived from the rhizome of the ginger plant, have been traditionally used to treat high cholesterol levels due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Zingiber officinale, including gingerols and shogaols, help to reduce inflammation in the blood vessels, improve lipid profiles, and prevent the formation of plaque in the arteries. By consuming Zingiber officinale teas, individuals can benefit from reduced triglyceride levels, improved HDL cholesterol levels, and a lower risk of cardiovascular disease. Regular consumption of Zingiber officinale teas may also help to improve overall cardiovascular health and reduce the risk of complications associated with high cholesterol.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 tablespoon of dried Ginger root (Zingiber officinale) and a cup of water.
Boil the water in a kettle or pot and let it cool for 1-2 minutes.
Add the dried Ginger root to the boiled water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the Ginger root.
Drink the Ginger tea 1-2 times a day, as needed, to help lower high cholesterol levels.
preparations.preparation_study: According to the study, Zingiber officinale teas for high cholesterol may be beneficial as they were found to significantly improve lipid profiles, including high density lipoprotein-cholesterol (HDL-C), which was found to increase by 0.850 mg/dL (WMD = 0.850, 95% CI: 0.018, 1.682, p = .045).
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Zingiber officinale teas can be used to treat high cholesterol ailments, but its consumption may cause side effects such as nausea, diarrhea, and stomach upset due to its active compound, gingerol. Precautions should be taken when using Zingiber officinale teas to treat high cholesterol, including reducing the dosage for pregnant women, people with bleeding disorders, and those taking anticoagulant medications to avoid interactions and complications.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Zingiber officinale tea twice daily.
Take 2-3 teaspoons of Zingiber officinale powder in 1 cup of water daily.
Use Zingiber officinale tea or powder for 3-6 months for noticeable effects.
Adjust dosage to 1 cup of tea or 1 teaspoon of powder daily if needed.
Consult doctor before making Zingiber officinale tea a regular part of your diet.

preparations.herb_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas, also known as licorice root tea, have been traditionally used to help treat high cholesterol levels due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents present in this herbal preparation, such as glycyrrhizin, flavonoids, and saponins, help to lower cholesterol levels and improve lipid profiles by inhibiting the absorption of dietary cholesterol and triglycerides. By consuming Glycyrrhiza glabra teas, individuals with high cholesterol may experience reduced levels of low-density lipoprotein (LDL) cholesterol, also known as "bad" cholesterol, and increased levels of high-density lipoprotein (HDL) cholesterol, or "good" cholesterol. The benefits of using Glycyrrhiza glabra teas to treat high cholesterol include improved cardiovascular health, reduced risk of heart disease, and enhanced overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Glycyrrhiza glabra roots. If using fresh roots, chop them into small pieces.
Combine 1 cup of Glycyrrhiza glabra roots with 4 cups of boiling water in a large pot. Let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a separate container. Discard the solids.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey to the tea, if desired, to sweeten it. Stir well to combine.
Drink 1 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day, preferably after meals, to help manage high cholesterol levels.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Acta pharmacologica Sinica", Glycyrrhiza glabra teas, also known as Gamichunggantang (GCT), have been found to reduce total cholesterol and triglyceride levels, and increase HDL-cholesterol levels in rats with high cholesterol.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas can be used to treat high cholesterol, but possible side effects may include increased blood pressure, potassium deficiency, and an irregular heartbeat due to its licorice root content, which can interact with the body's natural hormones. Precautions should be taken when using these teas, as they should be consumed in moderation and not used for extended periods, especially by individuals with pre-existing high blood pressure, kidney disease, or heart conditions.
preparation_usage: Consult a doctor before using Glycyrrhiza glabra teas for high cholesterol.
Take 1 cup of Glycyrrhiza glabra tea 2 times a day for 30 minutes.
Drink 1 cup of warm water before tea for better absorption.
Consume 2 tablespoons of licorice root powder as a supplement daily.
Monitor cholesterol levels regularly under doctor's guidance for 3 months.

preparations.herb_desc: Hypericum perforatum teas, also known as St. John's Wort, have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including high cholesterol. The bioactive constituents present in this herbal preparation, such as hyperforin and hypericin, help to treat high cholesterol by inhibiting the activity of low-density lipoprotein (LDL) receptors, thereby reducing the levels of bad cholesterol in the blood. The benefits of using Hypericum perforatum teas to treat high cholesterol include improved cardiovascular health, reduced risk of heart disease, and enhanced overall well-being. By consuming this herbal preparation, individuals can potentially lower their cholesterol levels and reduce their reliance on pharmaceutical medications, making it a natural and effective alternative for managing high cholesterol.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Hypericum perforatum flowers and 1 cup of boiling water.
Measure 1 tablespoon of dried Hypericum perforatum flowers into a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the Hypericum perforatum flowers and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into another cup using a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve to remove the flowers.
Drink 1-2 cups of Hypericum perforatum tea per day, ideally after meals, to help manage high cholesterol.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Hypericum perforatum teas can be used as an herbal remedy for high cholesterol, but it may cause side effects such as stomach upset, nausea, and interactions with blood thinning medications like warfarin. When using Hypericum perforatum teas to treat high cholesterol, it is recommended to monitor your liver function and avoid consuming it in large quantities, as excessive intake may lead to liver damage or other adverse effects.
preparation_usage: Consult a doctor before using Hypericum perforatum tea for high cholesterol.
Start with 1 teaspoon of dried Hypericum perforatum in 1 cup of boiling water.
Drink 1 cup of tea 2-3 times a day, before meals, for 2-3 weeks.
Gradually increase dosage to 2 teaspoons in 1 cup of boiling water, if needed.
Monitor cholesterol levels and adjust usage frequency as advised by your doctor.

preparations.herb_desc: Cassia auriculata teas are an herbal preparation that has been traditionally used to treat high cholesterol. The bioactive constituents present in this tea, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help to treat high cholesterol by inhibiting the activity of HMG-CoA reductase, an enzyme involved in the synthesis of cholesterol. The flavonoids and phenolic acids in Cassia auriculata teas also exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which contribute to the reduction of cholesterol levels and improvement of cardiovascular health. Regular consumption of Cassia auriculata teas has been shown to have various benefits, including lowering LDL cholesterol levels, improving lipid profiles, and reducing the risk of cardiovascular diseases.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Cassia auriculata leaves. You can buy them from a health food store or online.
Boil 1 cup of water in a pot. Remove from heat.
Add 1 tablespoon of dried Cassia auriculata leaves to the boiling water. Let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a strainer or cheesecloth. Discard the leaves.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help lower cholesterol levels. Consult a doctor before using it as a treatment.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Cassia auriculata teas can be used to treat high cholesterol, but they may cause side effects such as gastrointestinal issues, allergic reactions, and interactions with other medications. Precautions should be taken when using Cassia auriculata teas, including avoiding consumption in pregnant women, breastfeeding mothers, and individuals with liver or kidney problems, as well as monitoring blood sugar levels and blood pressure, and starting with small doses to assess tolerance.
preparation_usage: Consult a doctor before using Cassia auriculata tea for high cholesterol.
Drink 1 cup of Cassia auriculata tea 2 times a day.
Take 2 tablespoons of Cassia auriculata powder with 1 cup of water 2 times a day.
Continue usage for 3 months to see noticeable improvements in cholesterol levels.
Monitor your cholesterol levels regularly and adjust dosage as per doctor's advice.

preparations.herb_desc: Panax ginseng teas have been traditionally used to treat high cholesterol due to their properties as a vasodilator and antioxidant, which help to improve blood lipid profiles and reduce inflammation. This herbal preparation exerts its beneficial effects by inhibiting the activity of HMG-CoA reductase, a key enzyme involved in cholesterol synthesis, and by enhancing the expression of genes involved in cholesterol efflux, thereby promoting the removal of excess cholesterol from the bloodstream. The bioactive constituents of Panax ginseng teas, including ginsenosides, flavonoids, and polysaccharides, contribute to their cholesterol-lowering effects by modulating the activity of various enzymes and receptors involved in lipid metabolism. The regular consumption of Panax ginseng teas has been shown to have beneficial effects on blood lipid profiles, including reductions in total cholesterol, triglycerides, and LDL cholesterol levels, and an increase in HDL cholesterol levels, thereby reducing the risk of cardiovascular disease.
preparation_procedure: Boil 8 cups of water in a large pot.
Add 4-6 grams (about 1-2 teaspoons) of dried Panax ginseng root to a tea infuser.
Steep the ginseng root in the boiling water for 5-10 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the ginseng root.
Drink 1 cup of the ginseng tea 2-3 times a day, preferably after meals.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "The American journal of Chinese medicine", Panax ginseng teas for high cholesterol may help reduce plasma total cholesterol, triglyceride, and NEFA levels, while elevating plasma HDL-cholesterol and improving hepatic lipid profiles.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Panax ginseng teas can help reduce high cholesterol levels, but possible side effects may include dizziness, headache, insomnia, and stomach upset due to its stimulating properties. When using Panax ginseng teas to treat high cholesterol, it is essential to start with a low dose and gradually increase as needed, avoid consuming it before bedtime to minimize insomnia, and be cautious when engaging in activities that require alertness, such as driving or operating heavy machinery.
preparation_usage: Consult a doctor before using Panax ginseng tea for high cholesterol.
Take 1-2 cups of Panax ginseng tea per day, 1-2 times a day.
Start with a low dosage of 300-400 mg per day, and increase as needed.
Monitor your cholesterol levels regularly, and adjust your dosage if needed.
Combine Panax ginseng tea with a healthy diet and regular exercise for best results.

preparations.herb_desc: Curcuma longa teas, derived from the rhizomes of the turmeric plant, have been traditionally used to treat high cholesterol levels due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These teas help to treat high cholesterol by reducing the levels of low-density lipoprotein (LDL) cholesterol, also known as 'bad' cholesterol, and increasing the levels of high-density lipoprotein (HDL) cholesterol, also known as 'good' cholesterol. The bioactive constituents of Curcuma longa teas, such as curcumin, demethoxycurcumin, and bisdemethoxycurcumin, are responsible for their cholesterol-lowering effects, which help to improve overall cardiovascular health. Regular consumption of Curcuma longa teas has been shown to provide numerous benefits, including reducing the risk of heart disease, improving lipid profiles, and enhancing overall well-being, making it a valuable natural remedy for managing high cholesterol levels.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of water and 1 tablespoon of dried turmeric root (Curcuma longa).
Boil the water in a pot, then reduce heat to low.
Add 1 tablespoon of dried turmeric root to the pot.
Steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the tea into a cup.
Drink the tea 1-2 times a day, ideally after meals.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Indian journal of physiology and pharmacology", Curcuma longa teas for high cholesterol have been shown to elevate the HDL-cholesterol/total cholesterol ratio and exhibit better cholesterol and triglyceride lowering activity.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Curcuma longa teas can be an effective treatment for high cholesterol, but it may also cause side effects such as nausea, diarrhea, and stomach ulcers in some individuals. To use Curcuma longa teas safely, it is essential to start with a low dose and gradually increase it to avoid complications like bleeding or allergic reactions, and to also be cautious of potential interactions with other medications or medical conditions.
preparation_usage: Consult your doctor before using Curcuma longa tea for high cholesterol.
Take 1 teaspoon of Curcuma longa powder in 1 cup of hot water 2 times a day.
Drink the tea after meals, ideally after breakfast and lunch.
Continue using the tea for 3-6 months to see noticeable improvements.
Monitor your cholesterol levels regularly and adjust your dosage as needed.

preparations.herb_desc: Salvia miltiorrhiza teas, also known as Danshen, are a traditional Chinese herbal remedy that has been used to treat high cholesterol and cardiovascular diseases. The bioactive constituents of Salvia miltiorrhiza, including salvianolic acid, tanshinone, and rosmarinic acid, help to lower cholesterol levels and prevent the formation of blood clots by improving blood circulation and reducing inflammation. By reducing the levels of low-density lipoprotein (LDL) cholesterol and increasing the levels of high-density lipoprotein (HDL) cholesterol, Salvia miltiorrhiza teas can help to prevent the accumulation of cholesterol in the arteries and reduce the risk of cardiovascular disease. The benefits of using Salvia miltiorrhiza teas to treat high cholesterol include improved cardiovascular health, reduced risk of heart attack and stroke, and a lower risk of mortality from cardiovascular disease.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of water and 2-3 grams of dried Salvia miltiorrhiza root.
Bring the water to a boil in a pot and then reduce heat to medium-low.
Add the dried root to the water and let it steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea and discard the root. Add honey or sugar to taste if needed.
Drink 1/2 cup to 1 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day to help manage high cholesterol.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "BioFactors (Oxford, England)", Salvia miltiorrhiza teas for high cholesterol may be beneficial due to the presence of salvianolic acid B, a potent antioxidant that inhibits LDL oxidation and endothelial damage, potentially reducing the risk of atherosclerosis.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Salvia miltiorrhiza teas can be used to treat high cholesterol, but possible side effects may include dizziness, stomach upset, and allergic reactions due to its active compounds, such as salvianolic acid and tanshinone. Precautions to take when using Salvia miltiorrhiza teas include choosing a reputable supplier, following the recommended dosage, and being cautious of potential interactions with other medications or substances, and also being aware that it may worsen bleeding conditions due to its blood-thinning properties.
preparation_usage: Consult a doctor before using Salvia miltiorrhiza tea for high cholesterol
Take 1 teaspoon of Salvia miltiorrhiza tea powder with 1 cup of hot water twice daily
Drink tea 15 minutes before meals to help lower cholesterol levels
Continue using tea for 2-3 months to see noticeable improvements in cholesterol levels
Monitor cholesterol levels regularly and adjust tea dosage as advised by doctor

preparations.herb_desc: Lavandula angustifolia teas, derived from the lavender plant, have been traditionally used to treat high cholesterol levels due to their vasodilatory and anti-inflammatory properties. The herbal preparation helps to treat high cholesterol by improving blood flow, reducing inflammation in the blood vessels, and regulating lipid metabolism. The bioactive constituents, including linalool, linalyl acetate, and lavandulol, contribute to its cholesterol-lowering effects by inhibiting the production of low-density lipoprotein (LDL) cholesterol and increasing the levels of high-density lipoprotein (HDL) cholesterol. Regular consumption of Lavandula angustifolia teas has been shown to provide numerous benefits, including improved cardiovascular health, reduced risk of atherosclerosis, and enhanced overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of boiling water and 1 teaspoon of dried Lavandula angustifolia flowers.
Steep the flowers in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes, then strain the mixture.
Combine the strained liquid with 1 cup of boiling water and add 1 tablespoon of honey (optional).
Let the mixture cool and then refrigerate it for at least 2 hours.
Drink 1/2 cup of the cooled tea 2-3 times a day to help manage high cholesterol.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Lavandula angustifolia teas can be used to potentially lower cholesterol levels, but its use may also lead to side effects such as dizziness, nausea, and allergic reactions in some individuals. Precautions include using it in moderation, avoiding it if you have sensitive skin or allergies, and being cautious when taking it if you have a history of bleeding disorders, as it may interact with blood-thinning medications.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Lavandula angustifolia tea 2 times a day.
Take 1-2 teaspoons of dried herbs per cup of boiling water.
Consume for 2-3 months to see noticeable cholesterol reduction.
Monitor cholesterol levels regularly with doctor's guidance.
Maintain a balanced diet and regular exercise routine alongside tea consumption.

Regular consumption of Cinnamomum verum teas has been shown to be an effective natural remedy for managing high cholesterol levels and promoting overall cardiovascular well-being.

To avoid these side effects, it is recommended to consume Cinnamomum verum teas in moderation, ideally 1-2 cups per day, and to avoid mixing it with other medications or herbal supplements that may interact with its active compounds.

The herbal preparation works by reducing inflammation and improving blood lipid profiles, which in turn helps to lower cholesterol levels in the blood. The bioactive constituents of Urtica dioica tea, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, are responsible for its cholesterol-lowering effects, as they help to inhibit the absorption of dietary cholesterol and reduce the production of cholesterol in the liver. Regular consumption of Urtica dioica tea has been shown to benefit individuals with high cholesterol by improving overall cardiovascular health, reducing the risk of heart disease, and promoting a healthy weight.

Combine the leaves with 1 cup of boiling water in a saucepan.

Add honey to taste, if needed, and pour the tea into a cup.

preparations.preparation_side_effect: Urtica dioica teas can be used to help lower cholesterol levels, but it may cause side effects such as stomach upset, nausea, and diarrhea due to its diuretic properties and potential interaction with other medications. preparations.herb_desc: Cynara scolymus teas, derived from the artichoke plant, have been traditionally used to treat high cholesterol levels due to their unique properties that help to lower lipid profiles. The saponins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids present in Cynara scolymus teas are responsible for their cholesterol-lowering effects, which help to reduce the levels of LDL (bad) cholesterol and triglycerides in the blood. This herbal preparation works by inhibiting the absorption of cholesterol in the gut, reducing the synthesis of cholesterol in the liver, and improving the antioxidant status of the body, ultimately leading to a reduction in overall cholesterol levels. Regular consumption of Cynara scolymus teas has been shown to provide several benefits in treating high cholesterol, including improved cardiovascular health, reduced risk of atherosclerosis, and enhanced overall well-being.

Combine artichoke leaves with 2 cups of water in the saucepan.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the artichoke leaves.
Drink 1 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day to help lower high cholesterol.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Cynara scolymus teas can be used to help lower high cholesterol levels, but its prolonged consumption may lead to potential side effects such as stomach upset, diarrhea, and headaches. preparations.herb_desc: Centella asiatica teas have been traditionally used to treat high cholesterol due to their bioactive constituents, including triterpenoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which possess antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and lipid-lowering properties. The saponins present in Centella asiatica teas are believed to help reduce the levels of low-density lipoprotein (LDL) cholesterol and triglycerides in the blood, thereby improving cardiovascular health. The antioxidant properties of these teas help to prevent the oxidation of LDL cholesterol, which is a key factor in the development of atherosclerosis, a condition associated with high cholesterol. Regular consumption of Centella asiatica teas has been found to be beneficial in reducing the risk of cardiovascular diseases and improving overall lipid profiles in individuals with high cholesterol.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Centella asiatica leaves or 2 teaspoons of dried leaves.
Heat 1 cup of water in a pot until it boils.
Add 1-2 teaspoons of the Centella asiatica leaves to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes, then strain the liquid.
Drink 1 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day, sweetened with honey if desired.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Centella asiatica teas can be used to treat high cholesterol ailments, but possible side effects may include gastrointestinal issues, such as nausea, diarrhea, and stomach upset, as well as allergic reactions like skin rashes or itching. To use Centella asiatica teas safely, it is essential to monitor your body's reaction and adjust the dosage accordingly, and to avoid consuming it in excessive amounts, especially when combined with other medications.

preparations.herb_desc: Trifolium pratense teas, also known as red clover teas, have been traditionally used to treat high cholesterol due to their rich properties that help to lower lipid levels in the blood. The isoflavones present in red clover teas, specifically genistein and formononetin, have been shown to inhibit the activity of HMG-CoA reductase, an enzyme involved in cholesterol production. The consumption of Trifolium pratense teas has been associated with improved lipid profiles, reduced LDL cholesterol levels, and increased HDL cholesterol levels, ultimately contributing to the treatment of high cholesterol. The benefits of using Trifolium pratense teas for high cholesterol treatment include reduced risk of cardiovascular disease, improved overall cardiovascular health, and a natural approach to managing cholesterol levels without the side effects of pharmaceutical medications.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Trifolium pratense (Red Clover) flowers. You can buy them at a health food store or online.
Combine 1 cup of Red Clover flowers with 2 cups of boiling water in a large bowl or teapot.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes, then strain the liquid into a cup using a tea strainer or cheesecloth.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey to the tea, if desired, to help with flavor and taste.
Drink 1 cup of the Red Clover tea 2-3 times a day to support heart health and lower cholesterol levels.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Trifolium pratense teas can be used to treat high cholesterol, but it may cause side effects such as dizziness, headaches, and allergic reactions in some individuals. To use Trifolium pratense teas safely, it is essential to start with a low dose and gradually increase it, monitor blood pressure and blood sugar levels, and avoid consuming it if you have kidney or liver problems.

preparations.herb_desc: Salvia officinalis teas have been traditionally used to treat high cholesterol due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. They help to treat this ailment by reducing the levels of low-density lipoprotein (LDL) cholesterol in the blood and increasing the levels of high-density lipoprotein (HDL) cholesterol, thereby improving the overall lipid profile. The bioactive constituents of Salvia officinalis teas, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and rosmarinic acid, play a crucial role in their cholesterol-lowering effects, as they have been shown to inhibit the activity of enzymes involved in cholesterol synthesis and absorption. The benefits of using Salvia officinalis teas to treat high cholesterol include improved cardiovascular health, reduced risk of heart disease, and a natural and non-invasive approach to managing cholesterol levels.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Salvia officinalis leaves. You can use dried leaves if fresh ones are not available.
Measure 1 tablespoon of the gathered leaves and place them in a cup.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the leaves in the cup. Let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into another cup using a piece of cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve. Discard the leaves.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day, preferably after meals, to help lower high cholesterol levels.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Salvia officinalis teas can be used to treat high cholesterol due to its potential to lower lipid levels and improve overall cardiovascular health, but possible side effects may include nausea, dizziness, and stomach upset, especially when consumed in large quantities or for extended periods. Precautions should be taken to avoid using Salvia officinalis teas in combination with other medications, such as blood thinners, and to be mindful of potential interactions with other herbs, as well as to monitor blood sugar levels, as the tea may affect glucose metabolism.

preparations.herb_desc: Paeonia lactiflora teas, derived from the roots of the Paeonia lactiflora plant, have been traditionally used to treat high cholesterol levels due to their potential properties of reducing blood lipid profiles and inhibiting the activity of enzymes involved in cholesterol production. The bioactive constituents of Paeonia lactiflora teas, including paeoniflorin, paeonol, and paeonilactone, are believed to contribute to its cholesterol-lowering effects by suppressing the expression of genes related to cholesterol synthesis and enhancing the activity of enzymes involved in lipid metabolism. By regular consumption, Paeonia lactiflora teas may help to reduce total cholesterol and LDL (bad) cholesterol levels, thereby mitigating the risk of cardiovascular diseases associated with high cholesterol. The benefits of using Paeonia lactiflora teas as a complementary therapy for high cholesterol include a potential reduction in the reliance on pharmaceutical medications and a lower risk of side effects, making it a valuable herbal preparation for those seeking a natural approach to managing cholesterol levels.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 cups of fresh Paeonia lactiflora flowers and 2 cups of water.
Steep the flowers in the water for 5-7 minutes in a saucepan.
Strain the mixture and discard the flowers.
Add 2 tablespoons of honey to the liquid and stir well.
Drink 1 cup of the tea twice a day, after consulting a doctor or a healthcare professional.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Paeonia lactiflora teas can be used to potentially reduce high cholesterol levels, but possible side effects may include gastrointestinal issues such as nausea, diarrhea, and stomach upset due to the high content of anthraquinones. Precautions to take when using this herbal preparation include using it in moderation and not consuming it for extended periods, as prolonged use may exacerbate digestive problems and interact with other medications.

preparations.herb_desc: Crataegus monogyna teas have been traditionally used to help treat high cholesterol by reducing levels of low-density lipoprotein (LDL) or "bad" cholesterol. The antihyperlipidemic properties of these teas are attributed to their ability to inhibit the synthesis of cholesterol in the liver, thereby reducing overall cholesterol levels in the body. The bioactive constituents of Crataegus monogyna, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, are responsible for its beneficial effects on lipid metabolism. Regular consumption of Crataegus monogyna teas may also help to reduce the risk of cardiovascular disease by improving blood lipid profiles and enhancing overall cardiovascular health.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Crataegus monogyna leaves. You can use either the flowers, leaves, or bark for tea, but leaves are the most popular choice.
Use 1 tablespoon of the Crataegus monogyna leaves for every 1 cup of boiling water. You can adjust the amount to your taste.
Steep the leaves in boiling water for 5-7 minutes. You can let it steep for longer if you prefer a stronger tea.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or a piece of cheesecloth. Discard the leaves.
Drink 1-2 cups of the tea per day, ideally after meals, to help lower your cholesterol levels. Consult a doctor before using it as a treatment for high cholesterol.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Crataegus monogyna teas can be used to treat high cholesterol, but possible side effects may include dizziness, stomach upset, and allergic reactions due to the presence of tannins and flavonoids. Precautions should be taken when using these teas, such as starting with small doses and gradually increasing as needed, and being aware of potential interactions with other medications, especially blood thinners.

preparations.herb_desc: Vitis vinifera teas, derived from the grapevine, have been traditionally used to treat high cholesterol levels due to their rich content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The bioactive constituents, including resveratrol and quercetin, help to inhibit the activity of HMG-CoA reductase, a key enzyme in cholesterol synthesis, thereby reducing the levels of low-density lipoprotein (LDL) cholesterol. By modulating lipid metabolism and improving the ratio of high-density lipoprotein (HDL) to LDL cholesterol, Vitis vinifera teas help to alleviate the symptoms of high cholesterol, including reduced cardiovascular risk and improved overall health. The benefits of this herbal preparation include reduced inflammation, improved antioxidant defenses, and a decreased risk of cardiovascular disease, making it a promising natural remedy for managing high cholesterol levels.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 cups of dried Vitis vinifera leaves (also known as Grapevine leaves) or 4 cups of fresh leaves.
Measure 1 cup of water and bring it to a boil in a pot.
Add 1/2 cup of dried leaves or 1 cup of fresh leaves to the boiling water. If using fresh leaves, add 2 tablespoons of honey to mask the bitter taste.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes. Strain the mixture and discard the leaves.
Drink 1/2 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day to help lower cholesterol levels.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Vitis vinifera teas can be used to potentially lower cholesterol levels, but they may also cause side effects such as dizziness, nausea, and stomach upset in some individuals. When using Vitis vinifera teas to treat high cholesterol, it is essential to take precautions, including starting with a small dose, avoiding prolonged use, and monitoring blood sugar levels, as they may also lower blood sugar.

intro_study: According to "Avicenna journal of phytomedicine", teas for high cholesterol may be beneficial as a sour tea intake, rich in anthocyanins and inflation factor content, has been shown to decrease total cholesterol, HDL-C and LDL-C levels by up to 9.46%, 8.33%, and 9.80% respectively after 12 weeks.
